topic.what: Multi-tab website fingerprinting attack under traffic demixing scenario where encrypted traffic from K concurrent tabs must be identified
topic.how: DEMUX framework with overlapping boundary-preserving aggregation, multi-scale parallel CNN, and RoPE-enhanced two-stage Transformer encoder
topic.why_matters: Existing WF attacks degrade under multi-tab browsing; DEMUX addresses three structural requirements (R1-R3) that no prior method satisfies simultaneously, achieving state-of-the-art performance in closed-world, open-world, defense-augmented, and cross-configuration settings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Precision at K (fraction of ground-truth websites in top-K predictions)
characteristics.context: Closed-world 5-tab setting with Alexa Top-100 websites
Closed-world 5-tab setting with Alexa Top-100 websites

characteristics.what_it_measures: AUC (area under ROC curve) in open-world setting

characteristics.evidence: Table IV: TrafficSliver defense 2-tab P@2 of 0.940
characteristics.context: Two-tab setting with TrafficSliver defense (multi-path traffic splitting)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Epochs to exceed best baseline performance
characteristics.evidence: Section V-H: learning curve showing P@5 of 0.875 at epoch 40 surpasses ARES'25

good_for: multi-tab website fingerprinting attacks on Tor
evaluating WF defenses under concurrent browsing
plug-and-play improvement to existing WF pipelines
requires: Encrypted traffic packet direction and timestamp sequences
Known monitored website set for closed-world scenarios

novelty: Identifies three structural requirements (R1-R3) that any effective multi-tab demixing architecture must satisfy
Proposes overlapping window partitioning for boundary-preserving aggregation (R1)
Introduces multi-scale parallel CNN with heterogeneous kernel sizes (R2)
Applies rotary positional embedding for relative temporal association (R3)
Demonstrates plug-and-play Boundary Preserving Aggregation Module that consistently improves existing baselines
open_problems: Richer observation modalities beyond packet direction/timing
Longer session analysis and temporal dependencies
Stronger defenses specifically designed for multi-tab traffic
Active adversary capabilities for traffic manipulation
Real-world deployment evaluation with genuine Tor traffic
